---
name: ADR proposal
about: Surface a decision worth pinning in writing before code lands
title: "adr: "
labels: ["documentation"]
---

<!--
ADRs answer "what did we decide, why, and what we explicitly didn't choose"
so future changes can be reviewed against original intent. See:
- docs/adrs/README.md for conventions
- docs/core-principles.md "When to write an ADR"
- Existing ADRs as shape examples (especially 0001 for tone)
-->

## Decision to make

<!-- One sentence question. "Should we X or Y?" not "We should X." -->

## Context

<!-- What changed in the repo or the world that makes this decision live now? -->

## Drivers

<!-- The constraints / values that should weigh on the answer.
     Reproducibility, security, human-gated taste, agent ergonomics, etc.
     Cross-reference docs/core-principles.md when applicable. -->

## Options to consider

1. **Option A** — short description, pros, cons.
2. **Option B** — short description, pros, cons.
3. <!-- add as many as needed; include "status quo" if relevant -->

## Acceptance

- [ ] ADR drafted under `docs/adrs/NNNN-<slug>.md` (next free number)
- [ ] Row appended to `docs/adrs/README.md` index
- [ ] Cross-link from the most-affected doc page
- [ ] Follow-up issues filed if the decision changes existing code

## Out of scope

<!-- The decision itself — that's what the ADR is for. This issue is the prompt. -->
